channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Graham Platner faces calls to leave Maine Senate race after sexual assault allegation
item.description: Graham Platner is facing growing pressure to end his campaign for U.S. Senate in Maine after Politico reported an allegation of sexual assault. Platner has denied the claim.
item.content:encoded: Graham Platner is facing growing pressure to end his campaign for U.S. Senate in Maine after Politico reported an allegation of sexual assault. Platner has denied the claim.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: Meet 'Project 2029' — and its war on the annoyance economy
item.description: Remember Project 2025? Democrats are building their own governing blueprint, and one proposal takes aim at the "annoyance economy": robocalls, endless hold times, hidden fees and other everyday frustrations.
item.content:encoded: Remember Project 2025? Democrats are building their own governing blueprint, and one proposal takes aim at the "annoyance economy": robocalls, endless hold times, hidden fees and other everyday frustrations.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Trump's NATO pressure campaign continues as summit begins
item.description: President Trump's arrival in Ankara kicks off another potentially tense meeting for NATO.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's arrival in Ankara kicks off another potentially tense meeting for NATO.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: Heirs of 'odious' 167-year-old Supreme Court ruling see modern parallels
item.description: Descendants of Dred Scott and Chief Justice Roger Taney spoke about reconciliation at a church in the shadow of the Supreme Court last week as the high court wrestled with race and who can be an American.
item.content:encoded:
Descendants of Dred Scott and Chief Justice Roger Taney spoke about reconciliation at a church in the shadow of the Supreme Court last week as the high court wrestled with race and who can be an American.

(Image credit: Carrie Johnson)
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: As climate change damages streets and highways, the road ahead may be expensive
item.description: Heat waves are becoming more common and intense as a result of climate change — and roads are suffering as a result. Are the nation's roads up to meeting the challenge of a warmer, wetter future?
item.content:encoded:
Heat waves are becoming more common and intense as a result of climate change — and roads are suffering as a result. Are the nation's roads up to meeting the challenge of a warmer, wetter future?

(Image credit: Mark Felix)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Explosions rock Damascus, wounding 18, as French President Macron visits Syria
item.description: The French president's office said that Macron was safe and that his meeting with Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa would continue as scheduled.
item.content:encoded:
The French president's office said that Macron was safe and that his meeting with Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa would continue as scheduled.

(Image credit: Ghaith Alsayed)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Graham Platner faces calls to end his Senate bid after sexual assault allegation, NATO summit begins in Turkey, U.S. men's run at the World Cup ends with loss to Belgium.
item.content:encoded: Graham Platner faces calls to end his Senate bid after sexual assault allegation, NATO summit begins in Turkey, U.S. men's run at the World Cup ends with loss to Belgium.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: China test-launches a ballistic missile in the South Pacific and raises regional concerns
item.description: The launch, using a dummy warhead, took place the same day Australia and Fiji signed a mutual defense treaty meant to counter Chinese influence in the Pacific.
item.content:encoded:
The launch, using a dummy warhead, took place the same day Australia and Fiji signed a mutual defense treaty meant to counter Chinese influence in the Pacific.

(Image credit: Li Xiangchao)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Tanker set ablaze after being struck by projectile in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iranian state television said the tanker came under attack after ignoring warnings but did not directly claim the assault.
item.content:encoded:
Iranian state television said the tanker came under attack after ignoring warnings but did not directly claim the assault.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: What a divorce coach wishes couples knew before ending a marriage
item.description: Divorce is a tool, not a weapon, says Karen McNenny, author of a new book on the subject. She explains how to end a marriage while protecting your family and your mental health.
item.content:encoded:
Divorce is a tool, not a weapon, says Karen McNenny, author of a new book on the subject. She explains how to end a marriage while protecting your family and your mental health.

(Image credit: Wiley/Jossey-Bass/NPR, Nicole Wickens/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra
